meta.note: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH. Edit ONLY this file. All artifacts (website, CV .tex/PDF/DOCX, LinkedIn blocks, knowledge graph) are regenerated from it by `python build/build.py`. See README.md.
meta.standard: JSON Resume (jsonresume.org/schema) v1.0.0, extended with x_* namespaced fields for site-specific content.

basics.name: Nishanur Rahman
basics.label: Operations & Data Science Professional | AI/ML & LLM Automation
basics.summary: Operations and Data Science professional with 3+ years across AI/ML product development, LLM-powered automation, full-stack web applications, and operational analytics. Currently building Gen AI-driven quality systems at Amazon's Last Mile operations — including self-improving RAG architectures, real-time dashboards, and SLA engines. Passionate about translating complex operational problems into scalable, data-driven solutions that directly prevent customer-facing defects. Background in Mechanical Engineering with an ongoing BS in Data Science from IIT Madras.

work.name: Amazon
work.summary: Building Gen AI-driven quality systems for Last Mile operations — self-improving RAG assistants, real-time dashboards, and SLA engines that prevent customer-facing defects.

highlights.name: Resolve-It — LLM Powered Andon Cord
highlights.description: Built an LLM-powered Andon Cord enabling auditors to raise SOP doubts from GeoStudio in one click. System performs semantic retrieval against Policy 2.0 and verified responses, generates AI suggestions for POC validation, and delivers resolutions in ~5 minutes. Implements Human Feedback Reinforcement Learning — managers approve, reject, or edit responses daily, making the system self-improving. Impact: ~200 min/day recovered across 20 auditors.

highlights.name: Real-Time Revert Dashboard
highlights.description: Built an internal web application transforming manual quality reporting into an always-current intelligence platform. Managers upload daily revert case batches; system auto-identifies unique cases and computes role-aware KPIs on every page request — Pre-Appeal Quality, Post-Appeal Quality, Volume Trends, and Reason Distribution. Role-based views for auditors, managers, and skip-level leaders — eliminating ad-hoc reporting entirely.

highlights.name: Slack Workflow Based Andon Cord
highlights.description: Deployed a Slack-integrated anonymous doubt resolution workflow enabling auditors to raise SOP, process, and tool queries while maintaining complete anonymity. Each submission receives a unique Ticket ID, routed to managers with Answer, Assign, or Check Status options. Resolutions delivered via Slack DM and posted in team channel for organization-wide reference — ensuring consistent understanding of dynamic procedures.

highlights.name: AWS Hosted SLA Engine and Dashboard for Slack Workflow
highlights.description: Engineered an SLA tracking engine monitoring ticket lifecycle metrics — raise-to-resolution time, escalation rates, and manager approval timelines. Unresolved tickets beyond SLA are automatically escalated to leadership, ensuring zero unanswered queries. Monitoring dashboard provides leadership real-time visibility into resolution health and SLA compliance.

highlights.name: Project Prophesy
highlights.description: Built a proactive detection mechanism that flags repeat-defect addresses prone to escalation using multi-signal analysis, shifting defect handling from reactive to preventive.

highlights.name: Project Polygon
highlights.description: Reduced Shipping & Delivery Support (SDS) expenditure by enriching the learning datasets for Global Address Management (GAM) addresses that map to multiple delivery locations.

highlights.name: Project Place Based Geocoding
highlights.description: Merged the GAM and Geo-Tech address systems onto a single Place-Based Geocoding (PBG) learning model, eliminating the data dispersion caused by maintaining two disparate systems.

highlights.name: Data Mining
highlights.description: Corrected erroneous entries and augmented incomplete learned data points in the GAM machine-learning model, improving the completeness of its training data.

highlights.name: Data Sanitization
highlights.description: Sanitized inputs to the GAM Flywheel pipeline so the automated Address Resolution Process (ARP) receives clean, correctly structured data.

work.name: NPTEL — Joy of Computing using Python (Course id: noc24-cs57)
work.summary: Teaching assistant for a national online Python course delivered to 60,249 students.

highlights.name: Weekly Assignment
highlights.description: Developed comprehensive, challenging, and original assignments that extended beyond lecture content, introducing next-level concepts to build upon foundational material covered in class.

highlights.name: Daily Challenge Problem
highlights.description: Assessed the overall proficiency of the cohort and posed daily problems to encourage independent problem-solving, helping students realize the subject's potential and their ability to apply concepts effectively.

highlights.name: Daily Doubt Clearing
highlights.description: Provided one-on-one, ticket-based support to resolve student queries. Evaluated each student's grasp of concepts and tailored explanations to ensure proper conceptual understanding while discouraging rote learning.

highlights.name: Impact
highlights.description: Delivered course to 60,249 students, with 24,330 registrations and 12,432 successful certifications.

highlights.name: Served as HR Lead
highlights.description: Led event coordination, supervised automation tool development, managed hospitality, and maintained multi-level data records, ensuring transparency and efficiency.

highlights.name: Served as HR Associate
highlights.description: Leveraged event coordination and hospitality. Maintained multi-level data records and generated related reports.

highlights.name: Power Plant Engineering
highlights.description: Rigorously studied various Power Generation processes - from plant establishment to power generation.

x_hackathons.name: Resolve-It 2.0 — Self-Improving GenAI Andon Cord
x_hackathons.tagline: Conversational RAG assistant with live case context and human-in-the-loop ticketing
x_hackathons.status: Prototype submitted for review with the Data Governance team.

highlights.name: Live Conversational Bot
highlights.description: Enhanced Resolve-It into a chatbot embedded directly in GeoStudio that loads live case context, letting auditors converse for instant, SOP-grounded guidance — making the system faster and more robust than the one-click predecessor.

highlights.name: Grounded RAG + Guardrails
highlights.description: Answers are generated only from retrieved program SOPs, anecdotes, and manager-approved resolutions, with three layers of reliability — source grounding, contextual grounding checks, and confidence thresholds that route low-certainty responses to manager review.

highlights.name: Human-in-the-Loop Ticketing
highlights.description: A “Disagree” button raises a ticket the moment the AI's answer is unsatisfactory or beyond scope; manager-resolved tickets are re-ingested into the Knowledge Base with elevated retrieval priority, creating a continuous self-improving loop.

highlights.name: Impact
highlights.description: Designed to support 300+ auditors across 3 sites served by only 14 SPOCs, recovering ~50% of SPOC bandwidth (~7 FTEs / ~14,560 hours annually) and shifting error detection from downstream QC to the point of decision.

publications.name: Design of Photovoltaic System for School Library: A Case Study
publications.publisher: International Journal of Advanced Research in Engineering and Technology (IJARET)
publications.summary: IJARET, Vol. 7, Issue 5, 2016, pp. 07–17. Designed a stand-alone solar photovoltaic system for the SSET library at SHIATS; annual consumption of 7,127 units can be halved with energy-efficient appliances, with the system paying back in 9 years and yielding 16 years of free electricity over its 25-year life.

x_pipeline.title: This site builds itself.
x_pipeline.intro: This portfolio is generated from a single source-of-truth data file (data/resume.json) using a hub-and-spoke pipeline. One edit propagates to every surface — the website, my CV (PDF & DOCX), a knowledge graph, and LinkedIn-ready text — automatically on every push via GitHub Actions.
x_pipeline.principle: DRY — every piece of knowledge has a single, unambiguous, authoritative representation (Hunt & Thomas, The Pragmatic Programmer, 1999).

spokes.name: 7 HTML pages
spokes.detail: Rendered from Jinja2 templates with schema.org/JSON-LD structured data for SEO.

spokes.name: CV → PDF
spokes.detail: LaTeX generated from resume.json and compiled by TeX Live in CI.

spokes.name: CV → DOCX
spokes.detail: python-docx rendering, mirroring the PDF layout.

spokes.name: Knowledge Graph
spokes.detail: A property graph (schema.org + JSON Resume + ESCO ontology) regenerated from the same data.
